hero.headline:
The engineering
partner Europe's
enterprises
build with.
hero.subline: We ship the software, AI and digital products that European brands like Roche, BMW and ProSiebenSat.1 run their business on.

proof.kicker: Trusted by Europe's most demanding brands

c1.headline: We engineered the EV charging experience for 2.4M+ European drivers.
c1.body: In partnership with BMW's digital unit, alm delivered a unified charging and payment flow that replaced three legacy systems across 8 European markets.

c2.headline: We rebuilt the streaming infrastructure that 12M+ Europeans rely on monthly.
c2.body: A complete re-engineering of the content delivery platform — from monolith to event-driven architecture — handling peak loads across five broadcasting brands simultaneously.

c3.headline: We shipped the clinical data platform 4,000+ researchers depend on.
c3.body: A regulated, IEC 62304-compliant platform for clinical data management — engineered to meet the strictest EU MDR requirements while remaining fast enough for daily research workflows.

c1.desc: Scalable, secure web and mobile applications engineered for European enterprise requirements — from MVP to mission-critical production.

c2.name: AI Engineering
c2.desc: Custom LLM integrations, RAG architectures, and intelligent agents that ship into regulated production environments — not just PoCs.

eaa.label: An alm initiative
eaa.title: The EAA Compliance Checker.
eaa.body: The European Accessibility Act came into force in June 2025. We built a free tool that helps European companies assess where they stand — and what it takes to get compliant before enforcement begins.

companies have checked

p1.title: Why European automotive needs its own AI stack — and why importing one from Silicon Valley won't work.

p2.title: AI pilots don't fail on the model. They fail on deployment.

about.title:
An independent engineering firm.
Headquartered in Munich.
about.body: alm is not a creative agency. We are an engineering partner — independent from holdings, embedded in European enterprise reality, and accountable to outcomes that ship into production and stay there.

blog.desc: Deep technical writing on AI engineering, DSGVO compliance, European cloud infrastructure, and what it actually takes to ship software that European enterprises run on.

featured.title: DSGVO-compliant AI: What companies need to know in 2026 before going to production.
featured.excerpt: Between Article 22, the EU AI Act, and the first GDPR fines for AI systems, the regulatory landscape for enterprise AI has changed dramatically. A practical guide for software teams.

newsletter.title: Deep reads, no noise.
newsletter.sub: We publish when we have something worth saying. New articles on AI engineering, European compliance, and enterprise infrastructure — to your inbox.
newsletter.note: No frequency commitment. Unsubscribe anytime. DSGVO-compliant.
